title: Payment Card Tender System
summary: Contributed to the tender path that carries credit, debit, and gift card traffic across a nationwide store footprint and ecommerce, with observability and migration work on the critical path.

narrative.problem: Peak retail traffic and a zero-downtime mandate leave no room for opaque dependencies, weak telemetry, or brittle release procedures.
narrative.tradeoff: Favor observability-first design and buffered distributed flows over simpler synchronous coupling, even when that increases platform complexity up front.
narrative.decision: Worked within a Go and GKE payment environment to improve telemetry, support zero-downtime tender migrations, and make high-risk paths visible during production incidents.
narrative.outcome: Helped keep a platinum-tier payments domain measurable under load, with leadership-facing observability and sub-50ms p99 performance on critical paths.

architecture: Observability-first payment routing on distributed Go services

title: Magic Jungle Telemetry Mesh
summary: ESP32 environmental nodes feeding a Raspberry Pi 5 gateway, local vision workloads, and a public GCP analytics board for backyard growth telemetry.

narrative.problem: Owning sensors is not the same as operating a system. The goal is a resilient edge network with useful telemetry, not a pile of disconnected boards.
narrative.tradeoff: Keep sensing, buffering, and first-pass inference local for latency and resilience; ship curated events and long-horizon aggregates to GCP for analytics and a public dashboard.
narrative.decision: Use ESP32 nodes for capture, MQTT into a Pi 5 gateway for edge rules and Frigate, then normalize events in Cloud Run and persist time-series analytics in BigQuery.
narrative.outcome: Turns a backyard project into proof of silicon-to-satellite thinking: hardware, enclosures, observability, edge AI, and cloud analytics in one system.

architecture: Sensor mesh -> Pi 5 gateway -> Cloud Run control plane -> BigQuery analytics
cloud: Cloud Run, BigQuery, Cloud Monitoring, Terraform
hardware: ESP32 fleet, Raspberry Pi 5, cameras, 3D-printed enclosures
nextMilestone: Bring the first outdoor node online and publish a live telemetry board.

title: Edge Fleet Control Plane
summary: A control plane for Pi 5 and ESP32 hardware that manages heartbeats, signed OTA rollouts, incident timelines, and public health telemetry from one operator surface.

narrative.problem: A shelf of boards is inventory, not architecture. The missing piece is coordinated fleet health, rollout discipline, and observable failure handling.
narrative.tradeoff: Use a lightweight edge gateway and event bus instead of heavyweight cluster tooling so the system stays cheap, understandable, and deployable on real hobby hardware.
narrative.decision: Run fleet coordination and local buffering on the Pi 5, push signed rollout metadata and incident state through Cloud Run APIs, and store historical reliability signals in BigQuery.
narrative.outcome: Demonstrates that the same operational patterns used in enterprise systems can govern small edge fleets with real SLOs, rollback paths, and health visibility.

architecture: Edge heartbeat mesh -> Cloud Run fleet API -> BigQuery reliability timeline
cloud: Cloud Run, BigQuery, Secret Manager, Cloud Monitoring
hardware: Raspberry Pi 5, ESP32 nodes, smart relays
nextMilestone: Stand up signed firmware manifests and device heartbeat reporting.

title: Autonomous Print Cell
summary: A Bambu P1S production cell with ESP32 power and environmental sensing, Pi 5 vision analysis, and cloud-side quality analytics for failed-print detection.

narrative.problem: A printer is a tool. A print cell becomes a systems project only when failures, energy use, and quality signals are captured and acted on automatically.
narrative.tradeoff: Run vision and anomaly checks locally so detection remains fast and inexpensive; send structured events to GCP for quality trends, auditability, and remote monitoring.
narrative.decision: Pair the printer with ESP32 telemetry, local camera analysis on the Pi 5, and a Cloud Run ingestion pipeline that records print events, defects, and energy data into BigQuery.
narrative.outcome: Creates a hardware-backed AI operations case study that blends manufacturing telemetry, edge inference, and cloud analytics without becoming another generic web app.

architecture: Print telemetry + local vision -> Pi 5 event broker -> GCP quality analytics
cloud: Cloud Run, BigQuery, Looker Studio, Cloud Monitoring
hardware: Bambu P1S, ESP32 power monitor, Raspberry Pi 5, camera rig
nextMilestone: Instrument the printer job stream and calibrate local failed-print detection.

title: gimenez.dev Operator Console
summary: The portfolio itself as a live operator surface: recruiter-facing RAG, admin telemetry, trace-linked logs, and a war room that documents how the system behaves in production.

narrative.problem: Static portfolios do not answer technical questions, capture intent, or prove operational maturity.
narrative.tradeoff: Keep the app compact and cheap on Cloud Run while still exposing metrics, logs, admin workflows, and a truthful AI retrieval layer.
narrative.decision: Use Next.js 16 on Cloud Run with Terraform, Cloud Armor, structured logging, trace correlation, and a constrained RAG workflow backed by portfolio knowledge.
narrative.outcome: Useful as a lead-generation surface and as a supporting example of security, observability, and AI-as-infrastructure thinking.

architecture: Cloud Run operator console with recruiter analytics and RAG
cloud: Cloud Run, Cloud Logging, Cloud Trace, Terraform
hardware: Local inference hardware for the chat workflow

title: Churnistic
summary: Automated churn modeling and retraining pipeline built before the portfolio shifted toward edge systems and infrastructure proof.

narrative.problem: Model drift made predictions stale and retraining too manual.
narrative.tradeoff: Automate the ML loop and accept a narrower scope rather than overbuild the surrounding product surface.
narrative.decision: Used event-driven triggers, validation gates, and retraining automation to keep a churn model current.
narrative.outcome: Still a good ML automation story, but no longer the strongest evidence for a principal edge-to-cloud brand.

archiveReason: Strong automation work, but it does not reinforce the new silicon-to-satellite narrative.

title: Real-Time Trading Engine
summary: A streaming app with WebSockets and gRPC that reads as a capable engineering exercise but not a differentiated systems case study.

narrative.problem: Needed low-latency streaming data and live portfolio updates.
narrative.tradeoff: Optimize for real-time transport and service boundaries instead of deeper operational or hardware differentiation.
narrative.decision: Built a Go backend with WebSockets, gRPC, and PostgreSQL for real-time trading interactions.
narrative.outcome: Useful as a streaming exercise, but commodity finance demos are not the strongest signal for the current brand.

architecture: Microservices with gRPC and WebSocket streaming
archiveReason: The architecture is solid, but it looks like a product demo instead of a systems architecture headline.
